widgetData.source_sentence: deployment of ValleyRAT alongside additional payloads including keyloggers and cryptocurrency miners that establish persistent access
sentences: Adversaries rifle through virtual machine files and host configs seeking content to exfiltrate.
Credential interception happens via keyloggers that record keystrokes, often running unnoticed for extended periods.
Cyber adversaries weaponize existing account credentials to silently infiltrate, persist, and elevate privileges across enterprise systems.

widgetData.source_sentence: a Time of Check/Time of Use (TOC/TOU) vulnerability in the way libnvidia-container mounts files into the container.
sentences: Attackers deploy malware to scramble files and disks, crippling operations and extorting organizations for decryption keys.
Attackers leverage misconfigured S3 buckets to obtain credit card data.
Misconfigured bind mounts inadvertently hand attackers direct access to the host filesystem.

widgetData.source_sentence: These tokens contain cryptographic proof of successful authentication and can be replayed by attackers
sentences: Compromised credentials provide adversaries with authentic identities to hop across systems and reach high-value targets.
Compromised accounts allow token theft from managed identities for lateral movement.
Malicious software logs smart card PIN plus token to defeat MFA.

widgetData.source_sentence: Phishing-as-a-Service kits harvesting Microsoft 365 OAuth tokens
sentences: Exploit kits and crafted files turn trusted applications into launchpads for unauthorized remote code.
Adversaries abuse exposed cloud instances and edge appliances lacking robust host-based security controls.
Adversaries snatch Kubernetes tokens from breached container processes.

widgetData.source_sentence: Specifies 32-bit packet size larger than 1048576 bytes causing allocator failure.
sentences: Adversaries run benign loops or ping floods to waste time and exceed sandbox limits.
Tricking users into opening weaponized attachments triggers code execution via manipulated documents or executables.
Attackers exploit server software weaknesses, exhausting connections or memory to keep users locked out.
